message:
fix(channel/broadcast): drop stale buffer outside lock on subscriber reactivation

When a new subscriber is created after all previous receivers have been
dropped (receiver_count transitions 0→1), the stale buffer must be
cleared. Previously, buffer.clear() ran while holding the inner lock,
which could block other operations if the buffer contained items with
expensive Drop implementations.

Now uses std::mem::take to move the stale buffer out of the lock scope
into a local variable (_to_drop), which is dropped after the lock is
released. This preserves the same semantics (buffer is cleared before
the new subscriber can observe it) while avoiding holding the lock
during potentially expensive destructors.

Adds regression test using a DropBlocker that verifies:
1. The stale buffer's drop runs during subscribe (not deferred)
2. The inner lock is NOT held during the drop (try_lock succeeds)
3. The reactivated subscriber correctly receives subsequent messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
